_note: The shop's back room. Workshop participants build the locked baseline, then add shelf items à la carte. Edit this file to change the shelf — the studio reads it live on each open. tier drives the price-tag colour: free=no keys, one=one integration, many=several.

baseline.title: Your baseline
baseline.subtitle: Everyone builds this — same spine for the whole room

items.name: wiki-brain spine
items.what: The agent's memory — a folder of plain-markdown notes it reads and writes. Everything else reads from here.

items.name: chief-of-staff shell
items.what: The agent itself: its identity, your voice, and the read–decide–act loop. Capable, but does nothing special yet.

shelf.title: On the shelf
shelf.subtitle: Add what you want — read the price tag before you do

items.name: CRM
items.what: Records and recalls the people you meet — who they are, what they do, how you're connected, and your history with them.
items.brief: CRM — record and recall the people I meet as pages in my local wiki: identity, what they do, how we're connected, give/get, and our interaction history. One-hop network traversal. No external keys.

items.name: Daily debrief
items.what: A morning brief that sweeps your inbox, calendar and queues and tells you what to actually focus on today.
items.brief: Daily debrief — each morning, sweep my Gmail, Google Calendar and any queues, then write me a skimmable brief with proposed priorities. Warm to me; draft anything for sending in my own voice.

items.name: Interest radar
items.what: A daily, newsletter-style pulse on the topics you follow — 3–5 sourced, link-rich bullets on news, a sport, a company, a market, or a policy area.
items.brief: Interest radar — each morning, pull 3–5 sourced, link-rich bullets on the topics I follow (news, a sport, a company, a market, a policy area) using free RSS + web search, and fold them into my brief. No paid data, no keys.

items.name: Scheduling & email
items.what: Turn 'set up a call with Sam next week' into a drafted invite and email. You approve; it sends.
items.brief: Scheduling & email — interpret natural instructions like 'find time with Sam next week', resolve the person from my CRM, and draft the calendar hold and email in my voice. Propose, never auto-send.

items.name: Task list
items.what: A to-do list it manages for you — capture from chat, prioritise, and tick off, without you touching the tool.
items.brief: Task list — capture tasks I mention in conversation into Linear, list what's on my plate, reprioritise, and mark things done. Act immediately, then tell me what you did.

items.name: Image intake
items.what: Send it a screenshot; it reads it, works out what it is, and files it to the right place.
items.brief: Image intake — when I send a screenshot, view it, classify what it is (a task, a person, something to remember, or a question), and propose where to file it. Propose, don't file, until I confirm.

items.name: Inbox drain
items.what: Watches a channel and turns every message into a tracked item, on its own, every few minutes.
items.brief: Inbox drain — every few minutes, read new messages in my Discord inbox channel and turn each into a tracked Linear issue, mirroring replies back. Runs headless on a schedule. This is the agent's continuous spine.
